description_long: This house features a steeply pitched red gable roof with three prominent dormer windows on the front, each with white trim and gabled tops. The facade is symmetrical, with a central wooden door flanked by four shuttered windows, all set above a small set of black-and-white steps. Two large brick chimneys rise from either end of the house, adding to its classic appearance. The exterior walls are clad in light-colored horizontal siding, contrasted by dark shutters and a brown foundation. The rear and side views reveal additional dormers and windows, maintaining the symmetry and traditional design. The overall style is reminiscent of Colonial American architecture, with a focus on balance, proportion, and simple decorative elements.
description: A symmetrical Colonial-style house with a red gable roof, three front dormers, two brick chimneys, and a central entry with steps and shuttered windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry door, three dormer windows, four shuttered windows, and both chimneys.
description_view_1: Side view dominated by a large chimney, with dormers and windows visible along the roof and side wall.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with three dormers and four windows, but no visible entry door.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a large chimney, dormers, and side windows, similar to view 1.
attribute_entryway_design: Central wooden door with steps, flanked by shuttered windows, no porch or columns, simple and symmetrical.
